blocks.body: Future Perfect Continuous: Negatives — will not have been + V-ing for processes that will not still be continuing up to a future moment.

{"level":"B2","intro":"To make negatives in Future Perfect Continuous, we use will not have been + V-ing or the short form won't have been + V-ing.","rules":[{"label":"Negative form","color":"emerald","text":"The basic pattern is subject + will not have been + V-ing.","example":"I will not have been working long by noon."},{"label":"Won't have been","color":"blue","text":"In speech and normal writing, won't have been is very common.","example":"She won't have been waiting for an hour by then."},{"label":"Not continuing by then","color":"amber","text":"We use it to show that a process will not continue until that future moment, or will not have reached that duration yet.","example":"They won't have been living there for ten years by next summer."}]}

{"title":"1. How to build negatives","intro":"The form does not change with the subject. Only the negative after will changes.","items":[{"label":"Full form","title":"will not have been + V-ing","subtitle":"He will not have been working there for long by July."},{"label":"Short form","title":"won't have been + V-ing","subtitle":"We won't have been waiting for two hours by then."},{"label":"All subjects","title":"I / you / he / she / it / we / they","subtitle":"The structure stays the same in every case."},{"label":"Meaning","title":"not continuing or not lasting until then","subtitle":"The process will not continue until that point, or it will not have lasted that long yet."}]}

{"title":"2. When negatives are useful","sections":[{"label":"Not that duration by a deadline","color":"emerald","description":"Show that by a certain time or date, the process will not yet have lasted that long.","examples":[{"en":"By Friday, I won't have been working here for a month yet.","ua":"By Friday, a month of work will not have built up yet."},{"en":"By noon, she won't have been studying for three hours.","ua":"By noon, that duration will not exist yet."}]},{"label":"Not up to another future event","color":"blue","description":"The form fits when a process will not continue up to another future event.","examples":[{"en":"By the time you arrive, we won't have been waiting long.","ua":"By your arrival, the waiting still will not be long."},{"en":"When the guests come, he won't have been cooking all afternoon.","ua":"The process will not have continued that long up to the event."}]},{"label":"Plans or expectations will not be true","color":"amber","description":"Use it for a process that will not follow the expected timeline before a future limit.","examples":[{"en":"By next month, the team won't have been testing the feature for six weeks.","ua":"By that moment, the needed duration will not be there yet."},{"en":"By then, I won't have been learning Spanish for a year.","ua":"The expected duration still will not have arrived."}]},{"label":"No ongoing effort by then","color":"violet","description":"The negative works well when you want to stress the absence of a long process before a certain point.","examples":[{"en":"By summer, they won't have been training every day for long.","ua":"By summer, the regular process still will not be long."},{"en":"By 6 p.m., he won't have been driving all day.","ua":"We cannot say that the process will already have lasted all day by then."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Typical negative patterns","intro":"Look at the most common models.","rows":[{"en":"I won't have been working here for a year by July.","ua":"won't have been working","note":"the needed duration still will not have built up"},{"en":"She will not have been waiting long by then.","ua":"will not have been waiting","note":"the full form sounds more formal"},{"en":"We won't have been travelling all day when we arrive.","ua":"won't have been travelling","note":"the process will not have lasted that long up to another event"},{"en":"By next month, they won't have been testing it for six weeks.","ua":"won't have been testing","note":"the expected duration still will not be true"}],"warning":"After won't have been, use V-ing, not V1 or V3."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Leaving out been in the pattern.","wrong":"❌ She won't have waiting long by then.","right":"✅ She won't have been waiting long by then."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using V1 instead of V-ing.","wrong":"❌ We won't have been wait for two hours by noon.","right":"✅ We won't have been waiting for two hours by noon."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Putting not after have.","wrong":"❌ They will have not been working there for long by July.","right":"✅ They will not have been working there for long by July."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["Negatives in Future Perfect Continuous = will not have been + V-ing.","Short form: won't have been + V-ing.","The form shows that a process will not continue until a future moment, or will not have lasted that long yet.","After have been, always use V-ing."]}

{"title":"Future Perfect Continuous lessons","items":[{"label":"Forms and Use","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-forms","current":false},{"label":"Negatives","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-negatives","current":true},{"label":"Questions and Short Answers","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-questions","current":false},{"label":"Time Expressions","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-time-expressions","current":false}]}
